_provenance: V2.0 (2026-05-14): full voice-brief rewrite. All lines reauthored to Murrow voice spec (short declarative, no adjectives unless load-bearing, subtraction-mode, no reassurance, no editorializing, archive rule, corrected-question pattern). Address forms: Murrow and Asia use 'Dr. A. Cula' for the player; 'Mr. Pig' always; 'Mrs. Sikorska' for clients. V1.4 coffee reactions preserved with address-form correction (no expanded Doctor form). Mrożek aside planted in first_meeting per one-per-chapter budget. State IDs, triggers, and on_dismiss blocks unchanged from V2 for engine compatibility.

states.line: Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Pig is in his office. He will need ten minutes. He always needs ten minutes.

states._comment: Beat 4 — eviction briefing. The core Murrow introduction. Address-form invitation lands here. Mrożek aside (one per chapter) planted at the Policja reference. Sikorska case: 2015 renumbering, flat #7 → #12, landlord knowledge via 2019 renewal countersignature, motion to set aside, Friday 14:00 courtroom 4, retention pending.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mr. Murrow. Dr. A. Cula. I was told you'd have a case for me.

lines: Dr. A. Cula. Welcome. Noisy morning. The noise is not relevant to the case.
Sikorska eviction defense. Halina Sikorska, flat twelve. The notice was served to flat seven.
The building was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen. Seven became twelve. The landlord countersigned the two thousand and nineteen renewal at the correct address. He served the old one.
Ex parte hearing ran. The current resident at seven brought the notice down three weeks late. Actual notice reached her on the twenty-eighth of April.
We file a motion to set aside. Fourteen days from actual notice. We are inside the window.
Procedural binder. Second shelf, blue spine. The rights memo should be clipped inside the front cover. There is a Mrożek on the windowsill next to it. Leave that.
Motion hearing Friday at fourteen hundred, courtroom four.
She has not formally retained. She is coming in this afternoon.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The resident at number seven. Does his accepting the notice cure service?

lines: No. Express authority is required. He had none.
It is Murrow, to friends. The 'Mister' I keep for invoices.
I am still deciding whether reception staff qualify as friends or as workplace constellations.
Binder first. Memo second. Find me before fourteen hundred if anything reads wrong.

states.line: Binder. Good. Crab is at his desk. He will have checked the service clause already. He does that.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — S-grade. One word. Per the voice brief, this is immense praise from Murrow. No address form needed on a single-word turn.
V1.4 coffee reaction — D/F-grade. Uses the canonical 'Dr. A. Cula' address form.
states.line: Dr. A. Cula. That has procedural defects.

states.trigger: chapter1.has_law_binder == true && chapter1.has_rights_memo == true && chapter1.recruited_crab == true && chapter1.recruited_whimsy == true && chapter1.court_ready == false
states._comment: Beat 10 readiness check. Murrow runs the checklist. Pig wanders in with the printer-lease claim and the 'back in my day' beat. Murrow redirects. Readiness confirmed. Mrs. Borowski provisional title inherited from halina.json.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: The printer lease has come back as a kraken. We renew next month, presumably with bait.

lines: Crab and Whimsy confirmed for the appearance.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: When I was an apprentice, we had no readiness check. Only fear. We navigated by panic alone.

lines: Service first. Fair hearing second. Remedy last. We leave at thirteen-thirty.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Understood. Asia, has Mrs. Borowski been told we're coming?

lines.speaker: asia
lines.text: She rang an hour ago, Dr. A. Cula. She'll be there at quarter to.

idle_flavor: The paperwork is current. Whether it is correct is a different question.
Two pages are out of sequence. Not yours.
